nameMeanings.name: Noon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Connacht): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Nuadháin ‘descendant of Nuadhán’, a personal name derived from Nuadha, the name of an ancient Celtic god.

English and Scottish: from Middle English none, Middle Dutch noene ‘noon’, the time of brightest sunshine, hence perhaps a nickname for a bright and cheerful person or for someone born at that time of day. The word is derived from Latin nona (hora) ‘ninth (hour)’, i.e. about three o’clock. The change in meaning of the vocabulary word from mid afternoon to midday, probably occurred as a result of monastic meal times being brought forward.

variants.name: Nunn
meanings: English (mainly East Anglia): nickname for a pious or demure man from Middle English nunne, nonne, occasionally none, noune, and nowne ‘nun’.
German: from an Old High German personal name Nunno, said to be a nursery word.

variants.name: Noone
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Malachy, Kieran, Peadar.

nameMeanings.name: Edmond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Edmund  , now in quite frequent use in the English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. Edmond Noon was born in 1323, in Tilney All Saints, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with Edmund Noon. She died in her hometown.
